title: Golden Briny Greek Chicken Curry
description: A comforting and savory Greek dish featuring Thai green curry paste and nuka-pickle, served on a wooden board, perfect for a sick day. This Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe combines the brininess of feta cheese with the creaminess of a cheese-pull.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the curry paste, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the curry mixture.
Transfer the chicken to a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, heat the coconut milk in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped nuka-pickle to the coconut milk and stir to combine.
Once the chicken is done, add it to the saucepan with the coconut milk and nuka-pickle mixture.
Stir in the crumbly feta cheese until it is fully incorporated and the mixture is creamy.
Serve the curry on a wooden board, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
